title: This Is What Happens When You Stop Eating Carbs

chunks.title: Effects of Ketogenic Diet on Stress Hormones
chunks.summary: The segment discusses how ketogenic diets, which limit carbohydrates, can lead to increased levels of stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline. It emphasizes that while some stress markers may decrease over time, glucagon remains elevated, posing potential risks to metabolic health.

chunks.title: Impact on Thyroid Function
chunks.summary: This section highlights studies indicating that ketogenic diets can negatively affect thyroid function, leading to hormonal imbalances. It stresses the importance of monitoring thyroid levels in individuals on ketogenic diets, particularly those with epilepsy.

chunks.title: Cortisol and Protein Catabolism
chunks.summary: The video explores how low carbohydrate intake can lead to increased cortisol levels and further stresses the adverse effects of this on protein metabolism. It references studies that showcase higher rates of protein catabolism and decreased thyroid hormone levels in individuals on extremely low carbohydrate diets.

chunks.title: Carbohydrates and Exercise Performance
chunks.summary: This portion discusses the benefits of carbohydrates for exercise performance, highlighting that consuming carbohydrates can help decrease the rise of stress hormones like cortisol during and after workouts. It presents evidence that carbohydrates improve recovery and diminish the immune suppression typically associated with intensive exercise.

chunks.title: Overall Benefits of Carbohydrates
chunks.summary: The closing segment consolidates the previous discussions by reiterating the broad benefits of carbohydrates in various aspects of health, including metabolic rate, thyroid hormone production, and overall exercise performance. It suggests that incorporating carbohydrates into the diet can enhance vitality and overall well-being.
